content.method: Quasi-experimental design exploiting variation in local availability of 6/8-year tracks (distance/capacity discontinuities) combined with administrative panel data on school-level SES/Roma composition; complement with synthetic control comparison against Hungarian regions/cohorts without such tracks.
content.question: What is the independent causal effect of the 6-/8-year gimnázium track structure itself (age of selection) on between-school socioeconomic and Roma segregation, net of free school choice, residential sorting, and church/private admissions exemptions?
content.why_it_matters: Nearly every proposal's expected impact depends on how much of Hungary's segregation is attributable to the track structure versus other channels; without this, reforms may target the wrong lever entirely.
